new: docs/LLD/rpc.md (status note update only: scaffold -> proto contracts defined)
unchanged: engine/split/*.go (no gRPC wiring in this subtask, deferred to 3.2.3)
engine/graph/*.go, engine/catalog/*.go, engine/btree/* (contracts only reference these types by name in comments; no code changes)

direct: task-3.2.2 (engine/rpc/server.go) will implement the Go server side of the first 5 RPCs against these generated stubs
task-3.2.3 (engine/split/proposer_grpc.go) will implement a SplitProposer backed by the generated ProposeSplit client stub
task-3.2.4 (interceptor) will wrap the generated server/client with latency/cost interceptors
task-3.2.5 (integration test) exercises the full generated stack end-to-end
indirect: api/ (future HTTP gateway, per docs/LLD/rpc.md, not yet built) will eventually be a client of engine/rpc's server

risk_notes: Additive only: no existing Go or Python source file's behavior changes; only new files plus one new go.mod dependency pair and one doc status line.

Generated code is checked in (common Go community practice for this repo's tooling-light setup) rather than regenerated at build time, avoiding a new Makefile/CI codegen step in this subtask -- consistent with 'keep it minimal' guidance.

Message/RPC shapes are a best-effort mapping from existing Go signatures (GraphNeighbors, ReadPartial, SplitProposer) and could need small adjustment once 3.2.2/3.2.3 actually wire real handlers -- flagged, not blocking, since 3.2.1's acceptance criteria is compilation + shape match to docs/LLD/rpc.md's RPC list, not handler-level correctness.
